title: Failure to change top-module parameter value in hierarchy command with -chparam
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.27+22 (git sha1 53c0a6b78, gcc 12.2.0-3ubuntu1 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

I have a minimal example with the following SystemVerilog file ***module.sv*** which just uses two submodules:
```
module mod1 
  #(parameter NBITS=5)
   (input  logic [2*NBITS-1:0] A,
    output logic [2*NBITS-1:0] B);
   logic [2*NBITS-1:0] C;
   mod2 #(.NBITS(NBITS)) m (.A(A), .B(C));
   mod3 #(.NBITS(NBITS)) m (.A(C), .B(B));
endmodule

module mod2 
  #(parameter NBITS=5)
   (input  logic [2*NBITS-1:0] A,
    output logic [2*NBITS-1:0] B);   
   assign B = { A[NBITS-1:0], A[2*NBITS-1:NBITS] };
endmodule

module mod3
  #(parameter NBITS=5)
   (input  logic [2*NBITS-1:0] A,
    output logic [2*NBITS-1:0] B);   
   assign B = { A[NBITS-2:0], A[2*NBITS-1:NBITS], A[NBITS-1] };
endmodule
```
I want to load the file and then elaborate the top module ***mod1*** with a different value for the `NBITS` parameter.
```
/usr/local/bin/yosys -QT -p "read_verilog -defer -sv module.sv; ls; hierarchy -top mod1 -chparam NBITS 7"
```

### Expected Behavior

The module is elaborated correctly

### Actual Behavior

```
-- Running command `read_verilog -defer -sv module.sv; ls; hierarchy -top mod1 -chparam NBITS 7' --

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: module.sv
Parsing SystemVerilog input from `module.sv' to AST representation.
Storing AST representation for module `$abstract\mod1'.
Storing AST representation for module `$abstract\mod2'.
Storing AST representation for module `$abstract\mod3'.
Successfully finished Verilog frontend.

3 modules:
  $abstract\mod1
  $abstract\mod2
  $abstract\mod3

2. Executing HIERARCHY pass (managing design hierarchy).
Parameter \NBITS = 7

3. Executing AST frontend in derive mode using pre-parsed AST for module `\mod1'.
Parameter \NBITS = 7
Generating RTLIL representation for module `$paramod\mod1\NBITS=32'00000000000000000000000000000111'.
module.sv:8: ERROR: Cannot add cell `\m' because a cell with the same name was already created at module.sv:7.26-7.42!
```
Note that if the top module has only ***one*** submodule the procedure works correctly.
